category_title: Attention-seeking students without language disorders who mimic peers
minimal_context: The student is participating in a small group peer talk session to apply the word 'declared' from the book Rocket Writes a Story.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's start our small group discussion about the word 'declared' from the book Rocket Writes a Story.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Demonstrates a strong desire to share her thoughts spontaneously) I want to share my thoughts!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's listen to Helen first. Helen says her mom declared she had to do homework so she could watch SpongeBob and play with the dog.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Competes with other peers for attention) Listen to me, I have a sentence too!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay Sidney, what is your example for the word 'declared'?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Echoes Helen's response) My mom declared I would get homework done so that I could watch SpongeBob and play with the dog.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's start our small group discussion about the word 'declared' from the book Rocket Writes a Story.

STUDENT: (Demonstrates a strong desire to share her thoughts spontaneously) I want to share my thoughts!

TUTOR: Let's listen to Helen first. Helen says her mom declared she had to do homework so she could watch SpongeBob and play with the dog.

STUDENT: (Competes with other peers for attention) Listen to me, I have a sentence too!

TUTOR: Okay Sidney, what is your example for the word 'declared'?

STUDENT: (Echoes Helen's response) My mom declared I would get homework done so that I could watch SpongeBob and play with the dog.

task.content: Peer talk session about the word 'declared' during the reading of Rocket Writes a Story
task.learning_goal: Application of the words during peer discussions

context.materials: Rocket Writes a Story book, Bear's Loose Tooth book

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: After a peer shares their response during the times allocated for peer talk
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Repeats her peers' responses, echoing the specific example provided by the peer
related_quotes: My mom declared I would get homework done so that I could watch SpongeBob and play with the dog.

behavior_patterns.condition: When a peer gives an off-topic response during discussion
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Competes with other peers and corrects their responses
related_quotes: No, we are trying to speak anything about savoring.
